steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content:
Before the blank: This is a hypothetical designation — a complete clause.

After the blank: Proto-Indo-European is only postulated, its characteristics being inferred from the linguistic properties of its 448 daughters — another complete clause.

The conjunctive adverb however sits between them.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation pattern
steps.content:
Per the answer key, the right pattern is designation, however; — a comma before however and a semicolon after to join the second independent clause.

Choice A omits punctuation, creating a run-on; choice B splits into two sentences with a period; choice C (semicolon before, comma after) inverts the punctuation order used here.

The answer is D.
